engineIntelligence.h2: Jeep Renegade Engines - Codes, Common Failures & Replacement Costs
engineIntelligence.description: The cards below cover the main engines fitted to the Jeep Renegade in the UK, including documented failure causes, variant production years, and typical rebuilt engine costs based on current UK market data.

cards.altText: Jeep Renegade GSE-T4 engine - petrol 1.4L replacement UK
knownFailures: Timing chain tensioner failure - premature wear causes rattling noise and potential engine damage from 60k miles.
Turbocharger failure - codes P0236/P0299 common on high-mileage units with oil starvation issues.

cards.altText: Jeep Renegade FCA200 engine - diesel 2.0L replacement UK
knownFailures: EGR cooler cracking - updated cooler with revised baffling required to prevent coolant leaks and overheating.
Turbo actuator fault - electronic wastegate actuator failure causes reduced power and limp mode.

cards.altText: Jeep Renegade FCA160 engine - diesel 1.6L replacement UK
knownFailures: DPF regeneration issues - frequent short journeys cause filter blockage and warning lights on 1.6 units.
MultiAir valve control failure - hydraulic unit wear leads to rough idle and misfires on higher-mileage examples.

engineGuide.h2: Jeep Renegade Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Jeep Renegade across all UK generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 1.4L Petrol
entries.history: The 1.4L MultiAir turbo defined early Renegade petrol performance but became notorious for timing chain tensioner issues that drove UK replacement demand.
commonFailures: Timing chain tensioner wear (68369894AA) causing rattling from 60k miles
Turbocharger failure with codes P0236/P0299 due to oil starvation
MultiAir hydraulic unit wear leading to rough idle
entries.cta: Get quotes for GSE-T4 Jeep Renegade engine replacement

entries.title: 1.3L Petrol
entries.history: Introduced in 2020, the 1.3L FireFly replaced the 1.4L with improved efficiency and mild-hybrid capability for later Renegade models.
commonFailures: 48V belt-starter generator integration faults on mild-hybrid units
Turbo actuator electronic failures on higher-mileage examples
entries.cta: Get quotes for GSE-T6 Jeep Renegade engine replacement

entries.title: 1.0L Petrol
entries.history: The entry-level 1.0L three-cylinder launched in 2022 for budget-focused Renegade variants with lower running costs.
commonFailures: Limited long-term failure data; early reports of turbo lag on cold starts
entries.cta: Get quotes for GSE-T3 Jeep Renegade engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Diesel
entries.history: The workhorse 2.0L MultiJet II powered Trailhawk and higher-spec Renegades, valued for torque but prone to EGR cooler issues.
commonFailures: EGR cooler cracking requiring updated part with revised internal baffling
Turbo electronic wastegate actuator failure causing limp mode
DPF regeneration issues on short-journey vehicles
entries.cta: Get quotes for FCA200 Jeep Renegade engine replacement

entries.title: 1.6L Diesel
entries.history: Revised 1.6L MultiJet code introduced for 2022+ Renegade models with updated emissions hardware and improved reliability.
commonFailures: DPF blockage from frequent short trips causing warning lights and reduced performance
MultiAir valve control hydraulic unit wear on higher-mileage units
Early production units; limited failure data available to date
entries.cta: Get quotes for 46346020 Jeep Renegade engine replacement

commonProblems.h2: Jeep Renegade Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Jeep Renegade's 1.4L MultiAir and 2.0L MultiJet II engines dominate UK replacement enquiries, with timing chain tensioner wear and EGR cooler cracking being the most documented failure points. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty.

problems.h4: Timing Chain Tensioner Failure - Why Jeep Renegade 1.4L MultiAir Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.affectedModels: Jeep Renegade Sport, Longitude, Limited, Trailhawk (2015-2022, GSE-T4)
problems.rootCause: Original tensioner design (part 68369894AA) wears prematurely, allowing chain slack that causes rattling and potential valve damage.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace tensioner, guides and chain before catastrophic failure
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if caught early before chain jump; prevents major damage

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt unit with updated tensioner, bench tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for cars worth £4k+; eliminates repeat failure risk

problems.recommendation: On a Renegade 1.4 MultiAir valued under £5,000, a preventative tensioner replacement at 60k miles offers better value than waiting for failure. If the engine has already seized or thrown a code, a rebuilt unit with the updated tensioner is the only reliable solution.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt GSE-T4 engine prices for your Jeep Renegade

problems.h4: Turbocharger Failure - P0236/P0299 Codes on Jeep Renegade 1.4L Engines
problems.rootCause: Oil starvation or carbon buildup causes turbo bearing wear, triggering boost pressure sensor codes and loss of power.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Fit refurbished turbo with new actuator and oil lines
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if engine block is undamaged; requires thorough oil system flush

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit complete rebuilt unit with upgraded turbo, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if turbo failure caused oil contamination or bearing damage

problems.recommendation: If diagnostic confirms turbo failure without engine knock or metal debris in oil, a standalone turbo replacement can be cost-effective. However, if the engine has been run with low oil pressure, a full rebuild prevents repeat failure.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Jeep Renegade 1.4L turbo replacement

problems.h4: EGR Cooler Cracking - Jeep Renegade 2.0L MultiJet II Diesel Issue
problems.affectedModels: Jeep Renegade Longitude, Limited, Trailhawk 2.0 MultiJet II (2015-2022, FCA200)
problems.rootCause: Original EGR cooler develops internal cracks allowing coolant to enter exhaust, triggering overheating and emissions faults.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Fit revised cooler with improved internal baffling and new gaskets
repairOptions.longevity: Effective preventative fix; updated part addresses design flaw

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt diesel unit with updated EGR system, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best for high-mileage Trailhawk owners planning to keep vehicle long-term

problems.recommendation: For Renegade 2.0 diesel owners, fitting the updated EGR cooler at the first sign of coolant loss is far cheaper than waiting for engine damage. If the engine has overheated or shown white smoke, a rebuilt unit is the safer investment.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt FCA200 diesel engine prices for your Jeep Renegade

problems.h4: MultiAir Valve Control Failure - Jeep Renegade 1.4L & 1.6L Engines
problems.affectedModels: Jeep Renegade 1.4 MultiAir, 1.6 MultiJet II (2015-2022, GSE-T4/FCA160)
problems.rootCause: Hydraulic control unit seals degrade, causing oil pressure loss to valve lifters and resulting in rough idle, misfires or no-start.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace hydraulic control module and recalibrate system
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if engine block and timing components are sound

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit complete rebuilt unit with upgraded MultiAir system
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended for high-mileage vehicles or if valve damage has occurred

problems.recommendation: MultiAir unit replacement is often the right call if diagnostics confirm the fault is isolated to the hydraulic module. However, on engines over 100k miles, a full rebuild provides better long-term value and warranty coverage.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Jeep Renegade MultiAir engine repair

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Jeep Renegade
finalCta.paragraph: Whether you're facing timing chain rattles on a 1.4L MultiAir, EGR cooler issues on a 2.0L diesel, or MultiAir hydraulic faults, EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Jeep Renegade specialists. All rebuilt engines include upgraded components addressing the original failure point, plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide delivery. Compare prices today and get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Jeep Renegade Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Jeep Renegade and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

items.title: Jeep Renegade 1.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.0L GSE-T3 three-cylinder launched in 2022 as the entry-level petrol option for Renegade Sport and Night Eagle trims, prioritising fuel economy over outright performance.
commonFailurePoints: Limited long-term data; early reports of turbo lag on cold starts
items.cta: Compare Jeep Renegade 1.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Jeep Renegade 1.3L Petrol Engines
items.description: Introduced in 2020, the 1.3L FireFly (GSE-T6) replaced the 1.4L as the modern petrol choice, available with mild-hybrid technology for improved efficiency and lower emissions.
commonFailurePoints: 48V belt-starter generator integration faults on mild-hybrid variants
items.cta: Compare Jeep Renegade 1.3L petrol engine prices

items.title: Jeep Renegade 1.4L Petrol Engines
items.description: The original 1.4L MultiAir turbo (GSE-T4) defined early Renegade petrol performance but became the most searched-for replacement due to timing chain tensioner issues documented in Service Bulletin 09-005-17.
items.cta: Get quotes for Jeep Renegade 1.4L petrol engines

items.title: Jeep Renegade 1.6L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 1.6L MultiJet II (FCA160) arrived in 2018 as the efficient diesel option for entry and mid-spec Renegade trims, later updated to code 46346020 for 2022+ facelift models.
commonFailurePoints: DPF regeneration issues on short journeys; MultiAir hydraulic unit wear

items.title: Jeep Renegade 2.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.0L MultiJet II (FCA200) powered higher-spec and 4x4 Renegade variants, valued for torque but requiring attention to EGR cooler updates to prevent costly failures.
commonFailurePoints: EGR cooler cracking; turbo electronic actuator faults

items.title: Jeep Renegade Diesel Engines
items.description: Diesel Renegades (1.6L and 2.0L MultiJet II) dominated UK sales for torque and economy, but EGR cooler and DPF issues drive replacement enquiries.
items.descriptor: Diesel Renegades (1.6L and 2.0L MultiJet II) dominated UK sales for torque and economy, but EGR cooler and DPF issues drive replacement enquiries.
items.familiesLabel: Common Jeep Renegade Diesel Engine Families

FCA160 - 1.6L MultiJet II (Sport/Longitude), DPF regeneration issues on short trips
46346020 - Updated 1.6L code for 2022+ facelift models with revised emissions hardware
knownFor: Strong low-end torque ideal for UK motorway and off-road use

DPF warning lights on 1.6L engines used primarily for short urban journeys
High UK replacement demand on Trailhawk 4x4 variants

items.title: Jeep Renegade Petrol Engines
items.description: Petrol Renegades (1.0L, 1.3L, 1.4L) offer responsive performance but the early 1.4L MultiAir is notorious for timing chain tensioner failures.
items.descriptor: Petrol Renegades (1.0L, 1.3L, 1.4L) offer responsive performance but the early 1.4L MultiAir is notorious for timing chain tensioner failures.
items.familiesLabel: Common Jeep Renegade Petrol Engine Families
families: GSE-T4 - 1.4L MultiAir turbo (2015-2022), timing chain tensioner failure risk
GSE-T6 - 1.3L FireFly (2020-2024), mild-hybrid variants with 48V system complexity
GSE-T3 - 1.0L three-cylinder (2022+), entry-level option with limited long-term data
knownFor: 1.4L MultiAir delivers strong performance but requires timely tensioner maintenance

Turbo-related fault codes (P0236/P0299) common on high-mileage 1.4L units

items.title: Jeep Renegade Mild Hybrid (MHEV) Engines
items.description: Mild-hybrid Renegades (1.3L GSE-T6) use a 48V belt-starter generator to assist acceleration and enable start-stop, adding complexity to replacement decisions.
items.descriptor: Mild-hybrid Renegades (1.3L GSE-T6) use a 48V belt-starter generator to assist acceleration and enable start-stop, adding complexity to replacement decisions.
families: 48V belt-starter generator recovers braking energy to assist acceleration
Enables extended start-stop and coasting for improved fuel economy
Engine replacement requires hybrid system inspection and specialist calibration
foundIn: Renegade 4xe (European PHEV variant, limited UK availability)
importantNotes: MHEV engine replacement requires specialist with hybrid diagnostic equipment
48V battery health check recommended during any engine service
Availability of rebuilt MHEV units is more limited than standard engines

engineYears.h2: Jeep Renegade Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Jeep Renegade's UK production timeline, organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt engine prices from UK specialists.

years.preview: Launch Generation: 1.4L MultiAir & 2.0L MultiJet II Debut
years.description: The Renegade launched in the UK with the 1.4L MultiAir petrol and 2.0L MultiJet II diesel, establishing the engine lineup that would dominate replacement enquiries for years.
keyChanges: Renegade BU platform introduced to UK market with FCA Small Wide architecture
2.0L FCA200 MultiJet II diesel launched (140-170hp) with 9-speed automatic option
Trailhawk 4x4 variant introduced with unique suspension and engine calibration
knownFor: 1.4L MultiAir timing chain tensioner issues emerge in owner forums
Strong off-road capability of Trailhawk variant drives enthusiast ownership
Early 9-speed automatic calibration quirks affect drivability on some units
High UK demand for replacement engines begins as first units reach 60k miles
years.cta: Get quotes for 2015-2017 Jeep Renegade replacement engines
years.ticker: Jeep Renegade 1.4 timing chain replacement • Renegade 2.0 EGR cooler failure • GSE-T4 turbo fault codes P0236

years.description: The 1.6L MultiJet II diesel joined the lineup in 2018, offering a more efficient option for entry-spec Renegades while facelift updates refined the range.
keyChanges: 1.6L FCA160 MultiJet II diesel introduced for Sport and Longitude trims
Minor interior and exterior updates for 2019 model year
Updated infotainment and driver assistance features added
knownFor: 1.6L diesel praised for fuel economy but DPF issues reported on short-journey cars
Timing chain tensioner failures on 1.4L units become widely documented
Service Bulletin 09-005-17 confirms tensioner design update (68369894AA)
years.ticker: Renegade 1.6 DPF warning light • 1.4 MultiAir rattling noise engine • FCA160 MultiAir unit failure

years.description: The new 1.3L FireFly engine replaced the 1.4L MultiAir, introducing mild-hybrid technology and improved efficiency for later Renegade models.
keyChanges: 1.3L GSE-T6 FireFly engine launched with optional 48V mild-hybrid system
1.4L MultiAir phased out for new registrations but remains in high replacement demand
4xe plug-in hybrid variant announced for European markets (limited UK availability)
mainEngines: GSE-T6 - 1.3L petrol turbo with MHEV option (Limited/S/4xe)
FCA160 - 1.6L diesel turbo (continued for entry trims)
popularModels: Jeep Renegade Limited 1.3 T4 Mild Hybrid
knownFor: Mild-hybrid system adds complexity but improves real-world fuel economy
48V belt-starter generator faults emerge on early MHEV units
Replacement demand for 1.4L MultiAir remains highest due to installed base
years.cta: Enquire about Jeep Renegade 2020-2021 engine availability
years.ticker: Renegade 1.3 hybrid fault • GSE-T6 48V system error • Mild hybrid belt-starter replacement

years.description: The 2022 facelift (MP platform) introduced updated engine codes and refined emissions hardware for the final Renegade production years.
keyChanges: MP facelift platform launched with revised front/rear styling and interior
Updated 1.6L diesel code 46346020 introduced with improved emissions hardware
1.0L GSE-T3 three-cylinder added as new entry-level petrol option
Production ends for UK market in 2024
popularModels: Jeep Renegade Limited 1.3 T4 Mild Hybrid
Jeep Renegade Longitude 1.6 MultiJet II (updated code)
knownFor: Limited long-term failure data on updated engine codes due to recent introduction
Replacement demand shifts toward earlier BU-generation engines as they age
Final production models command premium for low-mileage used engines
years.cta: Get quotes for 2022-2024 Jeep Renegade replacement engines

engineYears.closing: Not sure which year your Jeep Renegade was built? Enter your registration number and we'll identify the exact engine, model year, and compatible replacement options instantly - all backed by a minimum 12‑month warranty from UK Jeep Renegade specialists.

faq.h2: Jeep Renegade Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does it cost to replace a Jeep Renegade engine in the UK?
items.answer: Replacing a Jeep Renegade engine typically costs £1,800-£3,400 for a rebuilt unit (supply only), depending on engine code and variant. Supply & fit adds £450-£850. Prices vary by damage severity and supplier location.
keyPoints: 1.4L MultiAir rebuilt: £1,800-£2,900 supply only
2.0L MultiJet II rebuilt: £2,100-£3,400 supply only
Supply & fit labour adds £450-£850 typically
All rebuilt units include minimum 12-month warranty
items.cta: Compare rebuilt Jeep Renegade engine prices for your variant

items.question: Is the Jeep Renegade 1.4 MultiAir engine reliable?
items.answer: The 1.4L MultiAir (GSE-T4) delivers strong performance but has a documented timing chain tensioner weakness. With preventative maintenance or a rebuilt unit with updated parts, it can be reliable long-term.
keyPoints: Timing chain tensioner (68369894AA) prone to premature wear
Service Bulletin 09-005-17 confirms design update available
Turbo failure codes P0236/P0299 common on high-mileage units
Rebuilt units with updated tensioner address the core issue
items.cta: Get quotes for Jeep Renegade 1.4L engine with updated tensioner

items.question: What causes Jeep Renegade diesel engines to fail?
items.answer: Jeep Renegade diesel failures typically stem from EGR cooler cracking (2.0L) or DPF blockage (1.6L), often linked to short-journey driving patterns. Updated parts and regular regeneration cycles mitigate risks.
keyPoints: 2.0L FCA200: EGR cooler cracking causes coolant loss
1.6L FCA160: DPF regeneration issues on urban-use vehicles
Turbo actuator faults affect both diesel engine codes
Updated EGR cooler with revised baffling prevents repeat failure
items.cta: Compare rebuilt Jeep Renegade diesel engine prices

items.question: Can I fit a used engine in my Jeep Renegade?
items.answer: Yes, used Jeep Renegade engines are available from UK breakers, but verify mileage, service history and compatibility. Rebuilt units offer better warranty coverage and address known failure points.
keyPoints: Used engines cost £900-£2,100 but have shorter warranties
Always request compression test results and donor vehicle history
Renegade-specific mounts and exhaust manifolds must match
Rebuilt units include updated parts targeting common failures
items.cta: Check used Jeep Renegade engine availability with warranty

items.question: How long should a Jeep Renegade engine last?
items.answer: With proper maintenance, Jeep Renegade engines can exceed 150,000 miles. However, the 1.4L MultiAir timing chain and 2.0L diesel EGR cooler require proactive attention to avoid premature failure.
keyPoints: Regular oil changes critical for MultiAir hydraulic system health
Diesel DPF requires periodic motorway runs for regeneration
Timing chain inspection recommended at 60k miles on 1.4L units
Updated parts extend lifespan of known weak components
items.cta: Find Jeep Renegade engine specialists for longevity advice

items.question: What's the difference between rebuilt and reconditioned Renegade engines?
items.answer: Rebuilt Jeep Renegade engines undergo comprehensive machining and component replacement, while reconditioned units focus on cleaning and replacing wear items. Both include warranty; rebuilt offers higher reliability.
keyPoints: Rebuilt: machining, new pistons/rings, upgraded failure-point parts
Reconditioned: cleaning, inspection, seal/gasket replacement
Rebuilt typically costs £200-£500 more but lasts longer
Both include minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty
items.cta: Compare rebuilt vs reconditioned Jeep Renegade engine options

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine on a 2015-2017 Jeep Renegade?
items.answer: For 2015-2017 Renegades valued under £5,000, engine replacement can still be cost-effective versus scrapping. A rebuilt 1.4L MultiAir at £1,800-£2,900 often extends vehicle life by 50k+ miles.
keyPoints: Compare engine cost (£1,800-£3,400) vs vehicle residual value
Rebuilt engine with warranty adds reliability beyond original spec
Consider selling as non-runner if replacement cost exceeds value
Supply & fit options simplify the process for non-mechanical owners
items.cta: Calculate repair vs scrap value for your Jeep Renegade

items.question: Do replacement Jeep Renegade engines come with a warranty?
items.answer: Yes, all rebuilt and reconditioned Jeep Renegade engines supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty. Terms vary by supplier; confirm coverage details before purchase.
keyPoints: Minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty standard on rebuilt units
Used engines typically have shorter 3-6 month warranties
Warranty covers parts and labour for manufacturing defects
Always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier
items.cta: Get quotes for Jeep Renegade engines with 12-month warranty

items.question: Where can I find the engine code on my Jeep Renegade?
items.answer: The Jeep Renegade engine code is stamped on the engine block near the oil filter housing and listed on the V5C registration document. Common codes include GSE-T4, FCA200, and FCA160.
keyPoints: Check V5C document section V.5 for engine code reference
Physical stamp located on block near oil filter housing
Enter registration on EngineMarket for instant code verification
Correct code ensures compatibility with replacement unit
items.cta: Identify your Jeep Renegade engine code instantly

items.question: Do you supply and fit Jeep Renegade engines nationwide?
items.answer: Yes, EngineMarket connects you with vetted UK specialists who supply and fit Jeep Renegade engines nationwide. Typical turnaround is 5-7 days, including delivery, fitting, fluids and road test.
keyPoints: Network covers all UK regions with Renegade-specialist garages
9-speed automatic bellhousing alignment expertise included
Supply & fit adds £450-£850 to engine price typically
All fitted engines include minimum 12-month warranty
items.cta: Find Jeep Renegade engine supply & fit deals near you
